Critical — act now
DATP Public Comment reading room is live. The City of Colorado Springs Digital Accessibility Transition Plan (Draft 2026), with the full plan embedded as a PDF and twelve structured public comments residents can adapt and submit. Comment portal links live in the reading room.
Public comment · COS Digital Accessibility Transition Plan · May 2026
Open the DATP reading room →
BOCC petition — service plan transparency & algorithmic pricing disclosure. A general resident petition with four narrow asks within existing BOCC authority. Sign and send.
Civic action · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
Open petition letter →
Veterans' petition to the EPC BOCC. Companion petition for veterans of any branch — service plan transparency and algorithmic pricing protections, framed around military housing harms.
Civic action · Veterans · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
Open veterans' petition →
LDC update — public comment on service plan transparency. A 300-word comment ready to submit through the El Paso County Land Development Code update process.
Public comment · LDC update · El Paso County · May 2026
Open LDC comment →
Background brief — algorithmic rent pricing in El Paso County. Sourced reference for journalists, policy analysts, and the COS Chamber housing committee: federal antitrust action, local policy options, and the case for BOCC-level service plan transparency.
Reference · Housing & pricing · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
Read background brief →
HOME-ARP comment window is live — closes May 26, 2026. COS is collecting public comment on the HOME-ARP Allocation Plan amendment via an ArcGIS Survey123 form Esri's own ACR rates as "Partially Supports" on WCAG 2.1 A/AA. A WCAG 2.1 AA Drupal Webform replacement is ready to install — bilingual EN/ES, JSON webhook + staff email + native storage, inline accommodation intake.
24 CFR 91.105 · ADA Title II · May 4, 2026
Open the accessible HTML form preview → Open the HOME-ARP bundle README →
Downtown Funding and Records Search Search BID assessments, DDA grants, TIF reimbursements, board meetings, and financial records in one place.

Open the search portal →
ADA Title II deadline: April 24, 2026 — 16 days. All local government websites must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Zero district sites are confirmed compliant on PDF documents.
HB21-1110 · 28 C.F.R. Part 35 · April 8, 2026
Open district audit dashboard →
249 of 284 metro districts have no website. CORA request is the only way a resident can access their own district's records.
EPC Assessor audit · April 2026
See district audit →
WS Districts (WSDM) serves 30+ districts on a single WordPress site. Last calendar entry: 2021. No governance docs. No financials. One compliance letter fixes all 30+ simultaneously.
Platform audit · April 2026
Draft compliance letter →
High priority
All posted PDFs likely fail WCAG 1.3.1. Meeting minutes, budgets, and audit docs are almost certainly untagged. This is the real HB21-1110 failure — not the homepage.
ADA document audit · April 2026
View ADA analysis →
0 of 8 audited sites post their service plan. C.R.S. §32-1-202 requires it. This is the foundational legal document. Not one district links it publicly.
Governance gap · §32-1-202
See audit dashboard →
Annual audit documents: publicly posted by 0 of 8 districts. Required by §32-1-106. Due to State Auditor by July 31. The clock is running.

See audit dashboard →
Informational
2 CORA requests pending response. Military housing numbers and district governance records both awaiting reply. Response window is still open.

View tracker →
Wolfersberger compliance and Bellamese analysis complete. New findings ready for review on developer agreements, compliance history, and parcel-level records.
Compliance analysis · April 2026
Read analysis →
Jurisdiction finder is live. Enter any El Paso County address to find your metro districts, school district, city jurisdiction, and utilities — plain language, no login.
New · April 2026
Find my jurisdiction →
CSPD Blotter — Weekly History is live. 271 incidents pulled directly from the live blotter feed, paired with a Poisson 95% confidence band to flag statistical anomalies week to week. Tabs are full APG-pattern keyboard accessible; every chart has an aria-label.
New · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open blotter dashboard →
Lorson Ranch financial dashboard is live. Seven cross-pledged metro districts, $94.2M aggregate audited deficit, IGA money flows, and a per-home tax calculator — sourced from 2026 budgets and 2024 audits. Companion plain-language briefing available as a download.
New · Special Districts · April 2026
Open Lorson Ranch dashboard →
CBI Missing Persons form — accessible rebuild is live. Resolves all seven critical/serious findings from the ADA audit: skip link, live regions, focus management, EN/ES localization, real-time validation messaging. Drops into the existing Drupal Webform infrastructure with optional Claude API routing.
New · State · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open CBI form rebuild →
LexisNexis ADAM Program dashboard — accessible rebuild is live. Audit and reference rebuild of the missing-children dashboard: APG-pattern tabs with roving tabindex and full keyboard nav, solid amber focus rings (3:1 against any surface), darker success-green for AA contrast, and "View Poster" placeholders converted from dead anchors to real buttons.
New · Private Sector · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA

Accessibility Audit · WCAG 2.1 Level AA

ADA Compliance Audit
CDPHE CoHID Mortality Dashboard

Audited: April 8, 2026  ·  Platform: Tableau Server (cohealthviz.dphe.state.co.us)  ·  Standard: WCAG 2.1 Level AA / DOJ Title II Rule

⚠ COMPLIANCE DEADLINE: APRIL 24, 2026

Executive Summary

The Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment (CDPHE) operates a public health mortality data dashboard at cohealthviz.dphe.state.co.us using Tableau Server. This audit was conducted on April 8, 2026 — 16 days before the DOJ Title II ADA final rule compliance deadline of April 24, 2026 — which requires all state and local government web content to meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA.

The audit identified 7 violations, of which 4 are Critical (Level A failures) and 3 are Serious (Level AA failures). The most significant impact is that the primary data content of this dashboard — the county map, all charts, and all interactive controls — is completely inaccessible to screen reader users.

This document includes both the full violation catalogue and a ready-to-deploy accessible HTML alternative that can be embedded on the existing CDPHE pages alongside or in place of the Tableau embed.

4 Critical
(Level A)
3 Serious
(Level AA)
0 Passes
(of 7 tested)
16 Days to
Deadline
01

Violation Catalogue

Critical

All data visualizations have no accessible text alternative

WCAG 1.1.1 Non-text Content (Level A)  ·  1.3.1 Info and Relationships (Level A)

The county map, age group bar chart, sex breakdown chart, marital status chart, homelessness chart, and trend line are all rendered as SVG elements with no aria-label, no role attribute, and no nested <title> element. A screen reader user encounters these as empty, unnamed regions.

Impact: The entirety of the data this dashboard exists to communicate is invisible to blind users and screen reader users. This is the single most consequential failure on the page.

✗ Current

<svg width="900" height="600">
  <!-- No title, no role,
       no aria-label -->
  <g>...county paths...</g>
</svg>

✓ Required fix

<svg role="img"
     aria-labelledby="map-title"
     aria-describedby="map-desc">
  <title id="map-title">
    Colorado county death counts, 2024
  </title>
  <desc id="map-desc">
    Choropleth map showing total deaths
    by county. El Paso: 5,516.
    Denver: 4,996. Jefferson: 5,002.
  </desc>
  <g>...county paths...</g>
</svg>
Critical

No skip navigation link

WCAG 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks (Level A)

There is no skip link allowing keyboard and screen reader users to bypass the toolbar and navigation chrome and jump directly to the data content. Keyboard users must tab through every toolbar button on every page load.

✓ Required fix — add as first element in <body>

<a class="skip-link"
   href="#main-content">
  Skip to main content
</a>

/* CSS */
.skip-link {
  position: absolute;
  top: -999px;
}
.skip-link:focus {
  top: 0;
}
Critical

Form controls have no accessible labels

WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships (Level A)  ·  4.1.2 Name, Role, Value (Level A)

The "Select a Cause of Death" dropdown and the year range slider both lack associated <label> elements. Neither has an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. Screen readers announce them only as "combobox" and "slider" with no context.

✗ Current

<select>
  <option>All causes</option>
  ...
</select>

<input type="range"
  min="2000" max="2024">

✓ Required fix

<label for="cause-select">
  Select a Cause of Death
</label>
<select id="cause-select">
  <option>All causes</option>
</select>

<label for="year-slider">
  Select Year (2000–2024)
</label>
<input type="range"
  id="year-slider"
  min="2000" max="2024"
  aria-valuetext="Year 2024">
Critical

Multiple images have no alt text (12+ instances)

WCAG 1.1.1 Non-text Content (Level A)

At least 12 <img> elements have no alt attribute at all — not even an empty one. Screen readers will either skip them silently or read out the image file path or URL, which is disorienting and meaningless.

✗ Current

<img>
<img>
<img>
/* no alt, no role, nothing */

✓ Required fix

/* Decorative images */
<img src="..." alt=""
     role="presentation">

/* Informative images */
<img src="..."
     alt="CDPHE logo">
Serious

Color is the sole encoding on the county map

WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color (Level A)

The county choropleth map uses only a light-to-dark blue gradient to encode death counts. Users who are colorblind, have low vision, or are viewing on low-quality displays cannot distinguish county values.

Impact: Affects approximately 8% of male users (red-green colorblindness) plus users with low vision or monochrome displays.

Fix: Add text labels directly on county regions (already partially present), ensure sufficient contrast between the lightest and darkest fill values (minimum 3:1 ratio), and provide a supplementary data table as a non-color alternative (see Section 02 below).

Serious

No data table alternative for chart content

WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships (Level A)  ·  Section 508 §1194.22(g)

When data is displayed visually, an accessible equivalent — such as a structured HTML data table — must be available. No table alternative exists for any chart on this dashboard. The data is inaccessible to anyone who cannot perceive the visual representation.

Fix: Add a "View as table" toggle adjacent to each chart. See the working example in Section 02.

Serious

Raw data access requires third-party login (Google account)

WCAG 2.1 Principle 4 — Robust  ·  ADA Title II Equal Access

The state's open data portal (data-cdphe.opendata.arcgis.com) requires a Google account to access the underlying dataset. This creates an authentication barrier for public data — excluding users who lack or decline Google accounts, raising identity and privacy concerns for a health dataset.

Impact: Researchers, journalists, advocates, and members of the public who need raw mortality data cannot access it without creating or providing a commercial third-party account.

Fix: Provide a direct, unauthenticated CSV/Excel download link from the CDPHE domain, or through the existing Data Request System at datarequest.dphe.state.co.us. The data request system should be prominently linked from the dashboard page itself.

02

Ready-to-Deploy Accessible Alternative

The following components can be embedded directly on the CDPHE death statistics page alongside or instead of the Tableau embed. They are fully WCAG 2.1 AA compliant, require no third-party dependencies, and load without authentication. The code is provided in Section 03.

Accessible Controls Demo

Properly labeled form controls with visible focus indicators — a direct replacement for Tableau's unlabeled inputs.

Select to filter all charts below
←

Accessible Data Table — Colorado Deaths by Age Group (2024, All Causes)

This table presents the same data as the "Age Groups" bar chart in the Tableau dashboard, in an accessible format that works with all screen readers, keyboard navigation, and assistive technology.

Colorado Total Deaths by Age Group, 2024 — All Causes Source: CDPHE Vital Statistics Program. Data represents county of residence.
Age Group Deaths (Count) % of Total
Under 1 year 296 0.7%
1 through 4 years 61 0.1%
5 through 14 years 99 0.2%
15 through 24 years 597 1.3%
25 through 34 years 1,196 2.7%
35 through 44 years 1,844 4.1%
45 through 54 years 2,525 5.7%
55 through 64 years 4,909 11.0%
65 through 74 years 8,621 19.4%
75 through 84 years 11,562 26.0%
85 and above 12,738 28.6%
Total 44,527 100%

What This Fixes (at a glance)

  • ✓ Skip navigation link present as first element in <body>
  • ✓ All form controls have visible, properly associated <label> elements
  • ✓ Year slider uses aria-valuetext to announce the year in words, not just a number
  • ✓ Data table uses <th scope="row/col"> for proper screen reader traversal
  • ✓ Table has a <caption> providing full context without needing surrounding text
  • ✓ Responsive: table restructures to a list format on mobile viewports
  • ✓ All focus states are visible (2px solid outline, 3px offset) — WCAG 2.4.11
  • ✓ Live region (aria-live) on year display so screen readers announce value changes
  • ✗ SVG charts still require Tableau-side fixes — cannot be patched from outside the embed
  • ✗ Raw data login wall requires CDPHE to add a direct download link
03

Recommended Remediation Timeline

By Apr 14
Add skip link + label all form controls. These are single-line HTML changes that can be injected via JavaScript into the Tableau embed wrapper. Estimated: 1–2 hours developer time.
By Apr 17
Add accessible data table alternative (use the code in this document) to the colorado-death-statistics page. Publish a direct CSV download link with no authentication required. Estimated: 2–4 hours.
By Apr 21
File a request with Tableau to enable SVG title/desc elements and ARIA roles on the server-hosted workbook. If Tableau cannot remediate in time, add a prominent banner above the embed linking to the accessible table alternative.
Apr 24
DOJ Title II ADA final rule compliance deadline. At minimum, the accessible table alternative and labeled controls must be live. Document remediation steps taken for compliance records.
Ongoing
Full Tableau remediation including SVG labels and color contrast. Consider migrating to an open-source charting library (Observable Plot, Chart.js) that natively supports ARIA on SVG outputs.
04

Accessible Data Access — Where to Direct Users Now

Accessible Paths to the Raw Data (No Login Required)

Data Request System: datarequest.dphe.state.co.us/drs-app/ — submit a request for mortality data in CSV or Excel. No Google account required.

Email: cdphe.healthstatistics@state.co.us — direct contact to the Vital Statistics program for data requests.

Official Death Statistics page: cdphe.colorado.gov/colorado-death-statistics — links to all three Tableau views and the data request system.

These paths should be prominently linked from the Tableau dashboard page itself, before the embed, so users who cannot access the visual dashboard have an immediate alternative.

Get email updates

We'll email you a plain-language summary with links to the source documents whenever this office posts new public-record material.
